summary: Closed/unmerged PR that decompiled three Castle stage functions in src/melee/gr/grcastle.c: grCastle_801CD960, grCastle_801CF750, and fn_801CFAFC. The PR body claimed ninja built cleanly and fuzzy_match_percent was 100.0 for all three. The main technical issue was intentionally retained pointer arithmetic around Ground/GroundVars layout because the available structs did not expose accurate named fields for the touched offsets; the author later noted the work was consolidated into other PRs.

key_files.role: Implemented three previously stubbed/unknown Castle stage functions with matching-oriented casts, offsets, and stack padding.
Added Item_GObj forward declaration include and replaced fn_801CFAFC UNK_PARAMS prototype with a typed callback signature.

what_changed: Replaced the grCastle_801CD960 placeholder with a loop over 12 entries, calling grLib_801C9B6C on byte offsets from Ground user data: (u8*) gp + 0xE0 + i * 0x14.

Replaced the grCastle_801CF750 placeholder with logic that chooses idx based on arg1 == 4, uses PAD_STACK(16), checks cd->x34_flags.b1234 == 1, shifts the Ground pointer by idx * 16, and adds arg3 as f32 to offset 0xD0.

Replaced the fn_801CFAFC placeholder with a function taking Item_GObj*, Ground*, Vec3*, HSD_GObj*, and f32; it writes 4 through a s16 cast to gp->gv.castle2.xC4 and conditionally calls ftLib_80086A4C when ftLib_80086960(gobj) succeeds.
Updated grcastle.h to include <melee/it/forward.h> and gave fn_801CFAFC a typed prototype instead of UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS.
The PR was not merged; an issue comment says it was consolidated into other PRs.
smart_moves: Preserved exact pointer arithmetic instead of forcing misleading struct field access where the current Ground/GroundVars definitions did not describe the observed memory layout.
Flagged the layout problem explicitly: offset 0xE0 was described in comments as currently typed like a 4-byte pointer while the code treats it as an inline array of 0x14-byte elements.
Used matching-specific details such as PAD_STACK(16), byte-pointer casts, f32 and s16 casts, and an explicit bitfield comparison to maintain the claimed 100% match.
Improved the header prototype for fn_801CFAFC enough to expose real argument types while leaving the function name generic because no semantic name was established.
decomp_lessons: When a matched function requires raw offsets into Ground or GroundVars, that can indicate a struct-layout gap rather than poor local style; avoid inventing field names until the shared layout is corrected.
If a stage-specific GroundVars area behaves like an inline array, model changes may need to happen in the shared struct/header layer before pointer math can be safely cleaned up.
For matching, small details like stack padding and cast width matter; PAD_STACK(16), s16 stores, and f32 arithmetic were all part of the submitted implementation.
Typed callback prototypes can require adding the correct forward header; here Item_GObj in grcastle.h required <melee/it/forward.h>.
Closed PRs may still contain useful matching tactics, but if the PR was consolidated elsewhere, future work should verify which version landed before reusing code directly.
naming_conventions: Castle stage functions used the existing grCastle_<address> naming convention, such as grCastle_801CD960 and grCastle_801CF750.
The unknown helper/callback retained the generic fn_801CFAFC name despite receiving a typed prototype.
Local variable names followed common decomp shorthand: gobj for GObj pointers, gp for Ground*, cd for CollData*, and idx for an index.
Header declarations retained address comments such as /* 1CFAFC */ before prototypes.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Looped with a do/while and i < 12 for grCastle_801CD960, matching calls to grLib_801C9B6C over 0x14-byte-spaced entries at Ground offset 0xE0.
Used (u8*) casts for byte-accurate pointer math on Ground pointers.
Inserted PAD_STACK(16) in grCastle_801CF750 for stack matching.
Compared (s32) cd->x34_flags.b1234 == 1 rather than using a looser truthiness check.
Performed the grCastle_801CF750 write through *(f32*) ((u8*) gp + 0xD0) after adjusting gp by idx * 16.
Stored 4 into gp->gv.castle2.xC4 through *(s16*) to match access width in fn_801CFAFC.
Conditionally called ftLib_80086A4C(gobj, (f32) grCs_804D6970->x4) after ftLib_80086960(gobj).

review_feedback.detail: Author initially said pointer math would be fixed soon.
Author/Claude later explained that replacing pointer math with struct field access was non-trivial because the Castle GroundVars struct lacked named fields at the needed offsets.
Specific layout concern noted: offset 0xE0 was described as typed as a 4-byte pointer while the code treats it as an inline array of 0x14-byte elements.
Specific layout concern noted: grCastle_801CF750 shifts the Ground pointer itself by idx * 16.
Final comment says the work was consolidated into other PRs.
No formal review comments were present in the provided slice.

follow_up_queries: Which later PR consolidated or merged the grCastle_801CD960, grCastle_801CF750, and fn_801CFAFC work?
Does the final Ground/GroundVars Castle struct now model the offset 0xE0 inline array of 0x14-byte elements?
Was the Ground pointer shift by idx * 16 in grCastle_801CF750 later represented with a named struct or kept as pointer arithmetic?
Did fn_801CFAFC keep the same typed Item_GObj/Ground/Vec3/HSD_GObj/f32 prototype in the merged version?
